articles.title: A New Kind of Battery That Charges Faster When It Gets Bigger
articles.summary: Scientists in Australia have made a special battery called a quantum battery. It uses tiny particles and light to store energy, which is different from regular batteries that use chemicals. This new battery can charge, hold, and release energy. The cool part is that it charges faster as it gets bigger, unlike normal batteries that don't change speed with size. The scientists built a small model to show how it works, and they hope to make bigger ones in the future. This could help store more energy for things like phones or cars, making them charge quicker and last longer. The research was done by a team from CSIRO, RMIT University, and the University of Melbourne, and they shared their findings in a science journal.

articles.title: Comedians Ask for Help to Keep Comedy Fun
articles.summary: Comedians in Britain are asking the government for more money to help new comedians. Comedy is very popular, but it does not get much support from the state. Big comedians sell out arenas, but small places where young comedians learn need help. Comedy leaders met with ministers to say comedy should be seen as an art form like music or theatre. This could help get more funding. The Culture Minister wants to work with comedians to find ways to help. Some comedians say comedy is risky, and clubs need money to try new acts. They believe comedy needs diverse voices to stay interesting. Without support, the next generation of comedians might not succeed.

articles.title: Jamie and Sophie's Family Show
articles.summary: Jamie Laing and Sophie Habboo are a couple who met on a TV show called Made in Chelsea. People have watched their relationship grow from dating to getting married. Now, they are having a baby and are sharing this special time in a new documentary called Raising Chelsea. The show follows them as they try to have a baby, go through pregnancy, and become parents. They film everything, even the birth, because they want to be real and honest with their fans. They use only one camera to make it feel like a home video, so they act naturally. Jamie says they show all moments, good and bad, because that's what real life is like.

articles.title: World Cup Clothes Get a Cool New Look
articles.summary: The World Cup is a big soccer tournament, and this year, the clothes players wear are changing. Instead of just matching shorts and socks, companies like Adidas and Nike are making kits that look like streetwear. Streetwear is casual clothes people wear every day, like jeans and trainers. Nike made a fun video with players wearing their kits with cargo trousers. Adidas showed their kits with flared trousers and micro-shorts in Los Angeles, with celebrities watching. They want fans to wear these clothes in their own lives, not just for soccer. This idea started long ago in the UK in the 1970s, when fans called casuals wore polo shirts and denim jackets instead of full team colors. Now, these styles are popular everywhere. Adidas has a special jersey for £80, and Nike has a Brazil collaboration jersey for £79.99. For the 2026 World Cup, some shirts are even cropped, which means shorter, to be more exciting for female fans. This shows how soccer fashion is mixing with everyday style.

articles.title: Super Material Gets a Big Boost with Neat Atoms
articles.summary: Scientists have made a special material called MXene much better at conducting electricity. MXenes are thin materials made from metals and carbon or nitrogen. Usually, when they are made, the atoms on their surface are messy and mixed up, which slows down electrons like bumps on a road. A new way to make MXenes, called the GLS method, uses salts and iodine vapor to put atoms in a neat and orderly way on the surface. This makes the material cleaner and smoother. For one type called titanium carbide MXene, this new method made it 160 times better at conducting electricity and let electrons move more freely. The neat atoms also help the material work better for things like absorbing radar waves or shielding electronics. This discovery helps scientists design better materials for technology.

articles.title: Stars Eating Planets: A Space Mystery
articles.summary: Scientists have discovered that old stars might be eating giant planets that orbit very close to them. When stars like our Sun get old, they run out of fuel and grow bigger, becoming red giants. This study looked at many stars and found that planets close to these red giants are often missing, which suggests the stars are destroying them. The researchers think this happens because of gravity pulling the planets inward, like a tug-of-war. This could happen to our solar system in billions of years when the Sun becomes a red giant, but Earth is safer because it is farther away. The study helps us understand what happens to planets as stars age.
